title: quisqualis conferta
intro: Quisqualis conferta, also known as the Rangoon Creeper or Creeping Mahua, is a tropical evergreen climbing plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. Quisqualis conferta is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Indian medicine, Ayurveda, to promote overall well-being.
